## Purpose

The {{ config.extra.organization }} Vendor/Third Party Service Provider Management Policy informs management, business owners, IT project teams and support staff of the {{ config.extra.organization }} requirements for vendor access to and holding of {{ config.extra.organization }}' information.

The policy defines vendor responsibilities for the protection of {{ config.extra.organization }} information and information systems. It is designed to fulfill {{ config.extra.organization }} fiduciary and regulatory duties to employees, customers, auditors, regulatory agencies, and governments.

## Scope

The {{ config.extra.organization }} Vendor Access Policy applies to all individuals and/or parties (hereafter Vendor) that are responsible for the installation of new {{ config.extra.organization }} Information System assets; maintain the operations and maintenance of existing {{ config.extra.organization }} Information Systems; who do, or may, allow vendor access for support, maintenance, monitoring and/or troubleshooting purposes; and/or hold {{ config.extra.organization }} information and data.

## Policy

1. Vendor access to {{ config.extra.organization }} information resources is granted solely for the work or purpose contracted and for no other purposes.
1. Vendors must comply with the {{ config.extra.organization }} Non-disclosure Agreement and any other applicable policies determined by the scope of work.
1. Depending on the vendor agreements and contracts the following specifications may apply:

   - The {{ config.extra.organization }} information the vendor should have access to. If, at the time of contract negations this is unknown or ambiguous, mention of this should be made in the agreement.
   - How {{ config.extra.organization }} information is to be protected by the vendor. A copy of the Vendor's Security and Privacy Policy should be made available to {{ config.extra.organization }} where appropriate.
   - Acceptable methods for the return, destruction or disposal of {{ config.extra.organization }} information in the vendor's possession at the end of the contract.
   - How Personal Identifiable Information (PII) that the Vendor may have access to, retain storage of, or in the course of the contract have any exposure to will be handled by the Vendor. At a minimum, the Vendor must adhere to relevant stipulations in this clause, have a written and acknowledged confidentiality agreement with {{ config.extra.organization }} and with its own employees, and maintain a standard encryption at rest storage policy. Preferably, the Vendor's ISO 27001:2022 certificate can be presented to {{ config.extra.organization }} if available.
   - Agreement that the Vendor must only use {{ config.extra.organization }} information and Information Systems for the purpose of the business agreement.
   - Any other {{ config.extra.organization }} information acquired by the vendor in the course of the contract cannot be used for the vendor's own purposes or divulged to others.

1. When appropriate, the vendor must provide IT Mgmt. with a list of all employee names working on the contract. The list must be updated and provided to IT Mgmt. within 24 hours of staff changes, wherever possible.
1. When appropriate, regular work hours and duties will be defined in the contract. Work outside of defined timeframes must be approved by the appropriate {{ config.extra.organization }} business owners
1. For vendor management agreements, the contract or agreement must dictate who initiates contact for accessing updated {{ config.extra.organization }} documents.
1. Upon termination of contract, or at the request of {{ config.extra.organization }}, the vendor will return or destroy all {{ config.extra.organization }} information and provide written certification of that return or destruction within 24 hours.
1. Upon termination of contract, or at the request of IT, the vendor must surrender all {{ config.extra.organization }} access cards, badges, and equipment immediately. Equipment and/or supplies to be retained by the vendor must be documented by authorized IT management.

Upon departure of a vendor or vendor employee from the contract for any reason, the vendor will ensure that all sensitive information is collected and returned to IT or destroyed within 24 hours.

## Procedures

1. As applicable, perform a risk assessment using the {{ config.extra.organization }} Vendor Risk Assessment.
1. After the request for transfer of information or a work assignment, use DocuSign to get the NDA.
   1. The signed NDA provided must be completed by both {{ config.extra.organization }} and the Vendor before proceeding.
1. Open a help desk ticket requesting access.
   1. The signed NDA agreement must be attached to the ticket.
   1. This is for tracking and review purposes.
1. Place the signed agreement in the appropriate folder (e.g., current or potential customer name, contractor)
1. If necessary, create an appropriate Vendor login to the {{ config.extra.organization }} resource (e.g., Trust Center)
1. If the vendor is performing vendor management for {{ config.extra.organization }}, place the NDA in the appropriate mutual customer folder in addition to the vendor's completed authorization form for the mutual customer.
1. If the vendor is performing vendor management for {{ config.extra.organization }}, set a reminder for an annual NDA renewal.

## Licensing

Appropriate Management and any necessary personnel A) reviews licensing annually, B) tracks changes in the IT Spending Checklist spreadsheet, C) tracks licensing in our Vendor Management system, D) compares any necessary license needs with hardware inventory, and E) works with Accounting to determine any cost reduction that can occur.

## Enforcement:

Controls include, but are not limited to, customer satisfaction surveys, keeping track of the frequency of occurrence of breaches, and monitoring activity on {{ config.extra.organization }} networks.

## Review

IT will review this policy annually review or as-needed basis (e.g., when new SOC reports are available or when there are significant changes at Company or third-party).

## Exceptions

Any exception to the policy will be approved and noted by IT Management.
